categories.label: War & strategy
categories.note: Capture, position and territory — soldiers, stones and kings on a grid of squares or lines.

categories.label: Hunt & siege
categories.note: Asymmetric games: a few strong pieces against a swarm of weak ones.

categories.label: Race & tables
categories.note: Dice-driven race games — counters around a track, ancestors of backgammon and ludo.

categories.label: Sowing
categories.note: The mancala family — sow and capture seeds around a board of pits.

categories.label: Alignment
categories.note: Make a line of three — the morris and merels games scratched the world over.

categories.label: Dice & lots
categories.note: Gambling with knucklebones, cubes, marked beans and spinning tops.

categories.label: Hand & guess
categories.note: Quick wagering games of the hand — nothing needed but nimble fingers.

games.name: Go
games.native: 圍棋 Wéiqí · 囲碁 Igo · 바둑 Baduk
games.tagline: The oldest game still played by its original rules — surround territory with stones.

games.name: Chaturanga
games.tagline: The Indian root of chess, shogi and xiangqi — the four arms of an ancient army.

games.name: Xiangqi
games.tagline: Chess across a river — generals confined to their palaces, cannons that leap to capture.

games.name: Ludus latrunculorum
games.tagline: Rome's game of soldiers — surround and capture on a grid of squares.

games.name: Petteia
games.tagline: The Greek war of pebbles that Plato compared to philosophy — and Rome turned into latrunculi.

games.name: Alquerque
games.tagline: The medieval ancestor of draughts — leap and capture along a five-by-five lattice.

games.name: Hnefatafl
games.tagline: A king and his guard break out as twice their number close in — the Viking game of escape.

games.name: Bagh-Chal
games.tagline: Four tigers hunt; twenty goats try to pen them in — a perfectly uneven duel.

games.name: Fox and Geese
games.tagline: One fox against a gaggle of geese — can the flock corner the predator?

games.name: Senet
games.tagline: Humanity's oldest board game — race your pieces past the houses of the dead.

games.name: The Royal Game of Ur
games.tagline: A 4,600-year-old race whose rules were read off a Babylonian clay tablet.

games.name: Tabula
games.tagline: The direct ancestor of backgammon — race fifteen men home on three dice.

games.name: Backgammon
games.tagline: Tabula's descendant, perfected in Persia — two dice, the doubling cube, and centuries of taverns.

games.name: Pachisi
games.tagline: India's great cross-and-circle race — Akbar is said to have played it with living pieces.

games.name: Patolli
games.tagline: The Aztec race played for stakes — and watched over by Macuilxochitl, god of games.

games.name: The Game of the Goose
games.tagline: The first commercial board game — a spiral of sixty-three squares, pure luck and peril.

games.name: Oware
games.tagline: West Africa's great game of sowing — scatter seeds and reap your opponent's row.

games.name: Bao
games.tagline: The deepest mancala of all — four rows, a special house, and a master's reputation at stake.

games.name: Toguz Korgool
games.tagline: The steppe's national mancala — nine pits, nine pebbles, and a 'mother hole' you can seize.

games.name: Nine Men's Morris
games.tagline: Form a mill of three and pluck an enemy off — the great medieval line game.

games.name: Terni Lapilli
games.tagline: Three pebbles in a row, scratched on a thousand steps from Rome to China.

games.name: Mū Tōrere
games.tagline: The only board game the Māori are known to have played — block your foe on an eight-pointed star.

games.name: Rota
games.tagline: A wheel of eight spokes — the round cousin of three-in-a-row.

games.name: Tali
games.tagline: Four knucklebones, four faces — throw for the Venus and avoid the Dog.

games.name: Hazard
games.tagline: The great gambling dice game of the medieval tavern — Chaucer's 'bones' and the parent of craps.

games.name: Dreidel
games.tagline: A four-sided top spun at Hanukkah — nun, gimel, hey, shin: nothing, all, half, put in.

games.name: Morra
games.tagline: Throw out fingers and shout the sum — the oldest hand game still played, from Rome to today.

games.name: Par Impar
games.tagline: Odd or even? A fistful of nuts and a guess — the simplest wager of all.
